skill.name: MicroPython C Code Review
skill.description: Automated code review rules for MicroPython C code based on analysis of commits from core contributors

rules.title: Use MicroPython memory allocation functions
rules.description: Use m_new, m_renew, and m_del for memory management instead of malloc/free. This ensures consistent memory tracking and debugging.

rules.title: Use mp_raise_* functions for exceptions
rules.description: Raise MicroPython exceptions using mp_raise_* functions with proper error types. This ensures consistent exception handling across the codebase.

rules.title: Use MP_ERROR_TEXT for error messages
rules.description: Wrap error message strings with MP_ERROR_TEXT macro for proper handling of compile-time error text storage.
rules.note: Check that mp_raise_* calls use MP_ERROR_TEXT for string messages

rules.title: Consider using configuration guards for optional features
rules.description: Use #if MICROPY_* preprocessor guards for optional features to reduce binary size on constrained devices.
rules.recommendation: Wrap feature flags with #if MICROPY_* guards

rules.title: Use MicroPython type definitions
rules.description: Use mp_obj_t, mp_int_t, size_t, and other MicroPython types for consistency and portability across platforms.

rules.title: Use static and const for encapsulation
rules.description: Mark internal functions and data as static, and use const for immutable data to reduce namespace pollution and enable optimization.
rules.note: Check that module-internal items are marked static

rules.title: Add inline comments for non-obvious code
rules.description: Include comments explaining intent for complex logic, memory optimizations, and MicroPython-specific decisions.
rules.note: Check for documentation of optimization trade-offs

rules.title: Validate buffer boundaries and sizes
rules.description: Always validate array bounds and buffer sizes to prevent buffer overflows on embedded systems with limited memory.
rules.note: Check for boundary validation before array/buffer access

rules.title: Use mp_obj_new_* helpers
rules.description: Create MicroPython objects using mp_obj_new_* helpers for consistent object construction.

rules.title: Prefer qstr for interned strings
rules.description: Use qstr (MP_QSTR_*) for interned strings to save memory and enable fast comparisons.

rules.title: Use mp_arg_* for argument parsing
rules.description: Use mp_arg_* helpers for consistent argument parsing and validation.

rules.title: Use mp_stream_* for stream protocol
rules.description: Use mp_stream_* helpers to implement stream protocol consistently across ports.

rules.title: Use MP_ROM_PTR/MP_ROM_QSTR for ROM tables
rules.description: Store constant pointers and qstrs in ROM with MP_ROM_PTR/MP_ROM_QSTR to reduce RAM usage.

examples.observed:
{ MP_ROM_QSTR(MP_QSTR_PHY_DP83867), MP_ROM_INT(PHY_DP83867) },
{ MP_ROM_QSTR(MP_QSTR_WLAN), MP_ROM_PTR(&mp_network_cyw43_type) },
{ MP_ROM_QSTR(MP_QSTR_STAT_IDLE), MP_ROM_INT(CYW43_LINK_DOWN) },

rules.title: Use mp_printf for diagnostics
rules.description: Prefer mp_printf/mp_vprintf for diagnostic output to integrate with MicroPython's print backend.

rules.title: Use MP_DEFINE_CONST_FUN_OBJ_* for function bindings
rules.description: Expose C functions to Python using MP_DEFINE_CONST_FUN_OBJ_* macros for consistent bindings.

rules.title: Use vstr for dynamic string building
rules.description: Use vstr_* helpers for incremental string construction to avoid repeated reallocations.
